journal_entry.description: Meal purchase using corporate credit card
journal_entry.rationale: Cannot determine correct account without knowing business purpose. Meals can map to different accounts depending on context: Meals and Entertainment (50% deductible client meals), Travel Expenses (employee travel meals), Office Expenses (working meals during meetings), Employee Benefits (staff meals), or Marketing Expenses (promotional events). Each has different tax treatment and deductibility rules under CRA guidelines.
clarification_questions: What was the business purpose of this meal? (e.g., client entertainment, employee working meal, team meeting, travel meal, etc.)

output.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase (credit card payable increases by $125)

credit_classifier.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase (credit card payable increases by $125)

output.reason: Meal expense = expense increase. The $125 meal is a business expense (likely meals & entertainment). The debit increases the expense account. The credit side (not classified here) would increase the credit card liability.
